历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for Physics Category was approximately 687 → 738 → 740 → 669 → 741. Over the past five years, it fluctuated within the range of 669–741, with a baseline of about 741 in 2025. In 2024, it dropped to 669 (the lowest in recent years), then rebounded to 741 in 2025, showing an overall oscillating pattern. The provincial enrollment plan for Physics Category was 43 in 2021, 56 in 2022, 57 in 2023, 58 in 2024, and 59 in 2025, increasing slightly year by year. The 2026 plan is 64, an expansion of about 8.5% compared to 2025, consistent with recent trends, which slightly alleviates competition but to a limited extent.

学校层次与声誉.title: School Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Nanjing University is a 985, 211, and Double First-Class university. In the Soft Science Chinese University Rankings from 2022 to 2026, it consistently ranks 6th (top 6 nationally), placing it among the top-tier universities, which strongly attracts high-scoring candidates. In 2026, its reputation evaluation is somewhat negative (due to incidents like the cafeteria king crab event and the lack of dormitories for professional master's students), but its Douyin (TikTok) popularity ranks 17th overall (high heat), with high short video exposure, reflecting sustained high attention from applicants. Historical admission anomalies show that in 2022, it attracted attention due to major group score differences, but no significant gaps were observed. Nanjing University has 25 disciplines rated A- or above, including Computer Science and Technology (A+), Electronic Science and Technology (A), Physics (A+), Chemistry (A+), Astronomy (A+), Atmospheric Science (A+), Geology (A+), Chinese Language and Literature (A+), Foreign Languages and Literature (A+), and Business Administration (A+), which highly match the Physics Category enrollment majors (Computer Science, Electronic Information, Artificial Intelligence, Mathematical and Physical Sciences, etc.). The strong disciplinary strength strongly attracts high-scoring candidates.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Core majors in Physics Category remain highly popular: Computer Science (2024), Electronic Information/Communication (76.86, high heat recommendation), Electrical Engineering (74.28, high heat recommendation), all of which are hot choices in recent years. In 2026, the number of Physics Category candidates in the province is approximately 347,000, an increase of about 8.4% from 320,192 in 2025, intensifying competition. Nanjing University is located in Nanjing, Jiangsu Province, a core city in the Yangtze River Delta, with strong regional appeal. The university's enrollment share in its home province (Jiangsu) is about 18–22%, while in Hunan Province, it is about 2.5% (2024 data), making it an inter-provincial enrollment without local preference advantages.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 741, the 2026 enrollment expansion of about 8.5% slightly alleviating competition, the increase in provincial candidates by about 8.4% intensifying competition, the strong appeal of Nanjing University's top-tier status and flagship majors (Computer Science, Electronics, etc.) to high-scoring candidates, and the high Douyin popularity reflecting sustained attention, it is expected that the minimum ranking for Physics Category in 2026 will slightly decrease compared to 2025. The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 789, with a range of 699–879.

major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_3:

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: If your ranking falls within the predicted range of 699–879, it is recommended to prioritize Tier 2 majors as the main choice, aim for Tier 1 flagship majors (Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science) as a stretch, and use Tier 3 majors as safety options. If your ranking is significantly better than the lower bound of the range (e.g., within 699), focus on aiming for Tier 1 popular majors. If your ranking is near the upper bound (around 879), it is advisable to prioritize Tier 3 safe majors, supplemented by Tier 2 moderate majors.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for History Category fluctuated narrowly around 194→198→178→187→187, with an overall range of 178–198 in recent five years, and a baseline of approximately 187 in 2025; in 2023 it dropped to 178 (lowest in recent years), and stabilized at 187 in 2024–2025, with minimal fluctuation; provincial History Category enrollment plans were 15 in 2021, 15 in 2022, 20 in 2023, 28 in 2024, and 22 in 2025, with an expansion to 28 in 2024 followed by a reduction to 22 in 2025; the 2026 plan is 29, an increase of about 32% compared to 2025, a significant expansion that somewhat alleviates competition.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Tier and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Nanjing University is a 985, 211, and Double First-Class university, consistently ranked 6th nationally in the Shanghai Ranking (Top 6 in China) from 2022 to 2026, placing it at the top tier, strongly attracting high-scoring liberal arts students; in 2026, its reputation is slightly negative (due to incidents like the 'Emperor Crab' in the cafeteria), but its Douyin (TikTok) popularity ranks 17th overall (high heat), with high short video exposure, reflecting rising attention from applicants; historical admission anomalies show that in 2022, there was attention due to major group score differences, but no significant gaps; Nanjing University has 25 disciplines rated A- or above, including Chinese Language and Literature A+, Foreign Languages and Literature A+, Business Administration A+, Law A, Sociology A, Philosophy A, Journalism and Communication A-, etc., which highly match the History Category enrollment majors (Humanities Experimental Class, Social Sciences Experimental Class, Economics and Management Experimental Class, Chinese Language and Literature, Law, etc.), with strong disciplinary appeal to high-scoring liberal arts students.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Popularity among core liberal arts majors is diverging: Law (2024), Chinese Language/Literature 60.0 (recommended), Teacher Training/Education 55.84 (divergent and wait-and-see), with overall liberal arts major popularity stable; in 2026, the provincial History Category examinee count is approximately 187,000, an increase of about 31.2% from 142,547 in 2025, significantly intensifying competition; Nanjing University is located in Nanjing, Jiangsu Province, a core city in the Yangtze River Delta, with strong regional appeal; its enrollment share in Hunan Province is about 2.5% (2024 data), indicating cross-provincial enrollment with no local preference advantage.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 187, the approximately 32% enrollment expansion in 2026 significantly alleviates competition, but the provincial History Category examinee count increases by about 31.2%, pushing competition higher, along with Nanjing University's top-tier status and strong appeal of its liberal arts, history, philosophy, economics, management, and law flagship majors to high-scoring students, and high Douyin heat reflecting rising attention, the expansion and examinee increase roughly offset each other. It is predicted that the minimum ranking for History Category in 2026 will slightly decline compared to 2025, with a predicted minimum ranking of approximately 228, and a range of 202–254.
